comment.diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,84 @@
+name: Rotating Domains Checker
+
+on:
+  # Automatic run daily at 02:00 UTC
+  schedule:
+    - cron: '0 2 * * *'
+  # Manual run with mode selection
+  workflow_dispatch:
+    inputs:
+      mode:
+        description: 'Run mode'
+        required: false
+        default: 'prod_live'
+        type: choice
+        options:
+          - ''              # Use config.yml setting
+          - 'prod_live'     # Production mode with changes
+          - 'prod_dry'      # Production mode without changes (git operations simulation)
+          - 'test_live'     # Test directory with changes
+          - 'test_dry'      # Test directory without changes (git operations simulation)
+
+      config-path:
+        description: 'Path to configuration file'
+        required: false
+        default: './rdc_config.yml'
+        type: string
+
+      filters-path:
+        description: 'Path to filters directory'
+        required: false
+        default: './'
+        type: string
+
+jobs:
+  check-domains:
+    name: Rotating Domains Checker
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+
+    permissions:
+      contents: write          # Required for commits
+      pull-requests: write     # Required for PR creation
+      actions: read            # Required for reading workflow info
+
+    steps:
+      - name: 📥 Checkout repository
+        uses: actions/checkout@v4
+        with:
+          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
+          fetch-depth: 1
+
+      - name: 🔧 Setup Node.js
+        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
+        with:
+          node-version: '24'
+          cache: 'npm'
+
+      - name: 🚀 Run Rotating Domains Checker
+        run: |
+          # Configure git user for commits
+          git config --global user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
+          git config --global user.name "github-actions[bot]"
+
+          # Clone Rotating Domains Checker repository (public, no token needed)
+          git clone https://github.com/Alex-302/RotatingDomainsChecker.git /tmp/checker
+          cd /tmp/checker
+          npm install
+          npm run build
+
+          # Return to filters repository and run checker
+          cd $GITHUB_WORKSPACE
+          node /tmp/checker/dist/index.js
+        env:
+          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
+          INPUT_CONFIG_PATH: ${{ github.event.inputs.config-path || './config.yml' }}
+          INPUT_MODE: ${{ github.event.inputs.mode || '' }}
+          INPUT_FILTERS_PATH: ${{ github.event.inputs.filters-path || './' }}
+
+      - name: 📋 Upload log file
+        if: always()
+        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
comment.body:
You can use the latest version of `actions/upload-artifact`

```suggestion
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v6
```

labels.name: A: Problematic
labels.description: Issues, which cannot be resolved easily

issue.body:
# Sorry. The website is infected by AdShield, Inc.

Unfortunately, It is complicated to resolve with a regular adblock rule because the website is being protected by service of Ad-Shield that is an anti-adblock company.

## Userscript Installation
Please block Ad-Shield advertisement by installing [tinyShield userscript](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield).
Please check its installation guideline written in English by visiting [this URL](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/blob/main/README.md).

If you can understand Korean language, please refer https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/blob/main/README.ko.md.

<details>
<summary>Detailed description for iOS users</summary>

1. Install [**Usercripts**](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/userscripts/id1463298887) app
2. Enable **Usercripts** extension in Safari settings
	* iOS 18+: `System settings` => `Apps` => `Safari` => `Extensions`
	* iOS 17 and older: `System settings` => `Safari` => `Extensions`
	Find **Usercripts**, enable it and allow `On other sites` permission
3. Open the [tinyShield ](https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@filteringdev/tinyshield@latest/dist/tinyShield.user.js) userscript URL in browser
4. Press the extensions icon in the address bar of Safari and select Userscripts 
5. Tap to install
6. In opened popup, scroll down and press Install button
7. Done.

</details>

## Still Have a Problem?
An issue is occurred when the userscript is applied, please report to [this issue tracker](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/issues).

Please block Ad-Shield advertisement by installing [tinyShield userscript](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield).
Please check its installation guideline written in English by visiting [this URL](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/blob/main/README.md).

If you can understand Korean language, please refer https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/blob/main/README.ko.md.

<details>
<summary>Detailed description for iOS users</summary>

1. Install [**Usercripts**](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/userscripts/id1463298887) app
2. Enable **Usercripts** extension in Safari settings
	* iOS 18+: `System settings` => `Apps` => `Safari` => `Extensions`
	* iOS 17 and older: `System settings` => `Safari` => `Extensions`
	Find **Usercripts**, enable it and allow `On other sites` permission
3. Open the [tinyShield ](https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@filteringdev/tinyshield@latest/dist/tinyShield.user.js) userscript URL in browser
4. Press the extensions icon in the address bar of Safari and select Userscripts 
5. Tap to install
6. In opened popup, scroll down and press Install button
7. Done.

</details>

## Still Have a Problem?
An issue is occurred when the userscript is applied, please report to [this issue tracker](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/issues).

labels.name: A: Problematic
labels.description: Issues, which cannot be resolved easily

issue.body:
# Sorry. The website is infected by AdShield, Inc.

Unfortunately, It is complicated to resolve with a regular adblock rule because the website is being protected by service of Ad-Shield that is an anti-adblock company.

## Userscript Installation
Please block Ad-Shield advertisement by installing [tinyShield userscript](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield).
Please check its installation guideline written in English by visiting [this URL](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/blob/main/README.md).

If you can understand Korean language, please refer https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/blob/main/README.ko.md.

<details>
<summary>Detailed description for iOS users</summary>

1. Install [**Usercripts**](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/userscripts/id1463298887) app
2. Enable **Usercripts** extension in Safari settings
	* iOS 18+: `System settings` => `Apps` => `Safari` => `Extensions`

comment.diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,84 @@
+name: Rotating Domains Checker
+
+on:
+  # Automatic run daily at 02:00 UTC
+  schedule:
+    - cron: '0 2 * * *'
+  # Manual run with mode selection
+  workflow_dispatch:
+    inputs:
+      mode:
+        description: 'Run mode'
+        required: false
+        default: 'prod_live'
+        type: choice
+        options:
+          - ''              # Use config.yml setting
+          - 'prod_live'     # Production mode with changes
+          - 'prod_dry'      # Production mode without changes (git operations simulation)
+          - 'test_live'     # Test directory with changes
+          - 'test_dry'      # Test directory without changes (git operations simulation)
+
+      config-path:
+        description: 'Path to configuration file'
+        required: false
+        default: './rdc_config.yml'
+        type: string
+
+      filters-path:
+        description: 'Path to filters directory'
+        required: false
+        default: './'
+        type: string
+
+jobs:
+  check-domains:
+    name: Rotating Domains Checker
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+
+    permissions:
+      contents: write          # Required for commits
+      pull-requests: write     # Required for PR creation
+      actions: read            # Required for reading workflow info
+
+    steps:
+      - name: 📥 Checkout repository
+        uses: actions/checkout@v4
+        with:
+          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
+          fetch-depth: 1
+
+      - name: 🔧 Setup Node.js
+        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
+        with:
+          node-version: '24'
+          cache: 'npm'
+
+      - name: 🚀 Run Rotating Domains Checker
+        run: |
+          # Configure git user for commits
+          git config --global user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
+          git config --global user.name "github-actions[bot]"
+
+          # Clone Rotating Domains Checker repository (public, no token needed)
+          git clone https://github.com/Alex-302/RotatingDomainsChecker.git /tmp/checker
+          cd /tmp/checker
+          npm install
+          npm run build
+
+          # Return to filters repository and run checker
+          cd $GITHUB_WORKSPACE
+          node /tmp/checker/dist/index.js
comment.body: If you want to develop your script more DevOps-like, you can create [a composite action](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/tutorials/create-actions/create-a-composite-action) with Typescript and npm.

issue.title: Duplicate rules in RussianFilter (specific.txt)

labels.name: A: In progress
labels.description: Work on the issue is in progress

issue.body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] I checked the documentation and found no answer;
- [x] I checked to make sure that this issue has not already been filed;
- [x] This is not an ad/bug report.

### Problem description


These rules appear multiple times in the list.

- [ ] ##.ads
- [ ] ##.branding
- [ ] ###bia-wrap
- [ ] ###bia-padding
- [ ] ##.bannergroup
- [ ] ###sideDynamicPopup
- [ ] ##.creative-placeholder-v2
- [ ] ##.top-banner
- [ ] ##.video__topline
- [ ] ##div[class*="-adv"]
- [ ] ##div[class*="banner"]
- [ ] ##div[class^="banner-top"]
- [ ] ##div[data-component="se-game-app"]
- [ ] ##div[id^="div_sf_"]
- [ ] ##div[id^="unit_"]
- [ ] ##noindex > div[align="center"]
- [ ] ||youtube.com/embed/$domain=

- [ ] #dle-content > noindex:has(> div.hblock > div[id*="ScriptRoot"])
```
comandir.com###dle-content > noindex:has(> div.hblock > div[id*="ScriptRoot"])
comandir.com#?##dle-content > noindex:has(> div.hblock > div[id*="ScriptRoot"])
```








### Proposed solution

Merge or remove rules if possible.

### Additional information

_No response_

comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

issue.title: xi.hydnoidrobles.qpon
issue.body:
### Issue URL (Annoyance) [https://xi.hydnoidrobles.qpon/igDeXWMpUpKHHTDqBTF/132711/?&pdc=M0XhuSnrdwDJE7eef41f12V7khDZRHXgp3cpAIevLpY¶m_4=mangaread¶m_5=LGtQCs46q53rRCWW](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fxi.hydnoidrobles.qpon%2FigDeXWMpUpKHHTDqBTF%2F132711%2F%3Fmd%%26pdc%3DM0XhuSnrdwDJE7eef41f12V7khDZRHXgp3cpAIevLpY%26param_4%3Dmangaread%26param_5%3DLGtQCs46q53rRCWW) ### Comment > When visiting mangaread.com, no matter which manga I open, if I click on the stars to give a rating for the manga or if I click on the bell icon on the top of the page (for the notifications), 7 out of 10 times it opens (in a new tab) and redirects to a page like: > https://xi.hydnoidrobles.qpon/igDeXWMpUpKHHTDqBTF/132711/?&pdc=M0XhuSnrdwDJE7eef41f12V7khDZRHXgp3cpAIevLpY¶m_4=mangaread¶m_5=LGtQCs46q53rRCWW > > I have created user rules like ||hydnoidrobles.qpon^, ||xi.hydnoidrobles.qpon^, |hydnoidrobles.qpon, |xi.hydnoidrobles.qpon, ||hydnoidrobles.qpon^$document and ||xi.hydnoidrobles.qpon^$document > > Yet the redirection still happens. > After creating the last two rules (with the $document), which I hadn't created initially, the redirection also still happens but instead of showing the black page like in the screenshot, it shows a message from adguard saying that it was blocked due to the user rule. > This is not my desired goal. > What I want to achieve is that adguard completely prevents the opening of (and redirection to) a new page. Which is the usual behavior, I mean usually when a webpage tries to open a different page the blocker immediately prevents that or at least immediately closes that page or window. ### Screenshots
Screenshot 1 ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/fd6j60219jk8scoo4gk4kgo8s0wkwc4d26dhj8bw3w.png?nc=1)

### Prerequisites

- [x] I checked the documentation and found no answer;

comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

issue.title: www.youtube.com

labels.name: T: Anti Adblock Script
labels.description: The site detects ad blocking

labels.name: NSFW
labels.description: Nudes and porn sites

labels.name: A: Problematic
labels.description: Issues, which cannot be resolved easily

issue.body:
# ATTENTION

> [!NOTE]  
> YouTube changes their detection scripts regularly. That means that sometime you can face the detection message.
> If all the following instructions do not work, please **email filters@adguard.com** or wait until any update is available. 

> [!CAUTION] 
> Please do not use another adblocker in the same time because using another adblocker can delay/lags the YouTube loading (e.g., AdBlock Plus and AdBlock)[^1] OR interfere AdGuard's filtering functionality.

> [!CAUTION] 
> Please do not use an userscript related to the bypassing YouTube detection message like [TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing](https://github.com/TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing).
> Recently, YouTube anti-adblock script added infinite dummy advertisements to try to neutralize the userscript. ( [TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing#264](https://github.com/TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing/issues/264) )

> [!WARNING]
> Please check that there no another web browser extension that has adblocking feature is enabled on your web browser. It can interfere AdGuard's filtering functionality.

> [!IMPORTANT]  
> Saving memory feature of your web browser or sleep feature of your operating system can interfere AdGuard's filtering functionality.
> In this case, please re-enable AdGuard's filtering by toggling the protection toggle.
> Following the instructions can be needed.

### The instructions
1. Force to update the filters by clicking the reload button in AdGuard Browser Extension/AdGuard mobile or update tray menu in your system tray. [^2]
2. Reload the YouTube webpage without cache by pressing <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>Shift</kbd> + <kbd>R</kbd> [^3].
3. Clear cache, cookies and site data of your web browser.
4. Re-open your web browser completely (checking your system monitor[^4] will be better).

[^1]: https://gitlab.com/adblockinc/ext/adblockplus/adblockplusui/-/issues/1576
[^2]: [AdGuard for Windows](https://adguard.com/kb/adguard-for-windows/overview/#tray-menu); [AdGuard for Mac](https://adguard.com/kb/adguard-for-mac/overview/#updates-check); 
[^3]: On macOS, <kbd>Command</kbd> + <kbd>Shift</kbd> + <kbd>R</kbd>
[^4]: Task Manager on Windows, System Monitor on GNOME Shell, KDE System Guard on KDE Plasma or Activity Monitor on macOS. 


Previous issue https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/issues/198973

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Annoyance) [https://xi.hydnoidrobles.qpon/igDeXWMpUpKHHTDqBTF/132711/?&pdc=M0XhuSnrdwDJE7eef41f12V7khDZRHXgp3cpAIevLpY¶m_4=mangaread¶m_5=LGtQCs46q53rRCWW](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fxi.hydnoidrobles.qpon%2FigDeXWMpUpKHHTDqBTF%2F132711%2F%3Fmd%%26pdc%3DM0XhuSnrdwDJE7eef41f12V7khDZRHXgp3cpAIevLpY%26param_4%3Dmangaread%26param_5%3DLGtQCs46q53rRCWW) ### Comment > When visiting mangaread.com, no matter which manga I open, if I click on the stars to give a rating for the manga or if I click on the bell icon on the top of the page (for the notifications), 7 out of 10 times it opens (in a new tab) and redirects to a page like: > https://xi.hydnoidrobles.qpon/igDeXWMpUpKHHTDqBTF/132711/?&pdc=M0XhuSnrdwDJE7eef41f12V7khDZRHXgp3cpAIevLpY¶m_4=mangaread¶m_5=LGtQCs46q53rRCWW > > I have created user rules like ||hydnoidrobles.qpon^, ||xi.hydnoidrobles.qpon^, |hydnoidrobles.qpon, |xi.hydnoidrobles.qpon, ||hydnoidrobles.qpon^$document and ||xi.hydnoidrobles.qpon^$document > > Yet the redirection still happens. > After creating the last two rules (with the $document), which I hadn't created initially, the redirection also still happens but instead of showing the black page like in the screenshot, it shows a message from adguard saying that it was blocked due to the user rule. > This is not my desired goal. > What I want to achieve is that adguard completely prevents the opening of (and redirection to) a new page. Which is the usual behavior, I mean usually when a webpage tries to open a different page the blocker immediately prevents that or at least immediately closes that page or window. ### Screenshots
Screenshot 1 ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/fd6j60219jk8scoo4gk4kgo8s0wkwc4d26dhj8bw3w.png?nc=1)
Screenshot 2 ![Screenshot 2](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/62o6gzyzw4kkwo0s408gowwwoc0c04ctsw24jh18dw.png?nc=1)

### Issue URL (Annoyance) [https://xi.hydnoidrobles.qpon/igDeXWMpUpKHHTDqBTF/132711/?&pdc=M0XhuSnrdwDJE7eef41f12V7khDZRHXgp3cpAIevLpY¶m_4=mangaread¶m_5=LGtQCs46q53rRCWW](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fxi.hydnoidrobles.qpon%2FigDeXWMpUpKHHTDqBTF%2F132711%2F%3Fmd%%26pdc%3DM0XhuSnrdwDJE7eef41f12V7khDZRHXgp3cpAIevLpY%26param_4%3Dmangaread%26param_5%3DLGtQCs46q53rRCWW) ### Comment > When visiting mangaread.com, no matter which manga I open, if I click on the stars to give a rating for the manga or if I click on the bell icon on the top of the page (for the notifications), 7 out of 10 times it opens (in a new tab) and redirects to a page like: > https://xi.hydnoidrobles.qpon/igDeXWMpUpKHHTDqBTF/132711/?&pdc=M0XhuSnrdwDJE7eef41f12V7khDZRHXgp3cpAIevLpY¶m_4=mangaread¶m_5=LGtQCs46q53rRCWW > > I have created user rules like ||hydnoidrobles.qpon^, ||xi.hydnoidrobles.qpon^, |hydnoidrobles.qpon, |xi.hydnoidrobles.qpon, ||hydnoidrobles.qpon^$document and ||xi.hydnoidrobles.qpon^$document > > Yet the redirection still happens. > After creating the last two rules (with the $document), which I hadn't created initially, the redirection also still happens but instead of showing the black page like in the screenshot, it shows a message from adguard saying that it was blocked due to the user rule. > This is not my desired goal. > What I want to achieve is that adguard completely prevents the opening of (and redirection to) a new page. Which is the usual behavior, I mean usually when a webpage tries to open a different page the blocker immediately prevents that or at least immediately closes that page or window. ### Screenshots
Screenshot 1 ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/fd6j60219jk8scoo4gk4kgo8s0wkwc4d26dhj8bw3w.png?nc=1)

### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [https://news.jp/i/-/tags/%E6%94%BF%E6%B2%BB?unit=39166665832988672](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fnews.jp%2Fi%2F-%2Ftags%2F%25E6%2594%25BF%25E6%25B2%25BB%3Funit%3D39166665832988672) ### Comment > When DNS filtering is enabled, tapping “もっと見る” (See More) will not display additional content. comment.body:
Maybe we can use scriptlets to fix it?
For example (order matters, `emptyObj` must be first added):
```adblock
news.jp#%#//scriptlet('set-constant', 'nor', 'emptyObj')
news.jp#%#//scriptlet('set-constant', 'nor.pageview', 'noopFunc')
news.jp#%#//scriptlet('set-constant', 'nor.eventURL', 'noopFunc')
news.jp#%#//scriptlet('set-constant', 'nor.pageviewURL', 'noopFunc')
news.jp#%#//scriptlet('set-constant', 'nor.setPageData', 'noopFunc')
```

issue.title: www.youtube.com

labels.name: T: Anti Adblock Script
labels.description: The site detects ad blocking

labels.name: NSFW
labels.description: Nudes and porn sites

labels.name: A: Problematic
labels.description: Issues, which cannot be resolved easily

issue.body:
# ATTENTION

> [!NOTE]  
> YouTube changes their detection scripts regularly. That means that sometime you can face the detection message.
> If all the following instructions do not work, please **email filters@adguard.com** or wait until any update is available. 

> [!CAUTION] 
> Please do not use another adblocker in the same time because using another adblocker can delay/lags the YouTube loading (e.g., AdBlock Plus and AdBlock)[^1] OR interfere AdGuard's filtering functionality.

> [!CAUTION] 
> Please do not use an userscript related to the bypassing YouTube detection message like [TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing](https://github.com/TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing).
> Recently, YouTube anti-adblock script added infinite dummy advertisements to try to neutralize the userscript. ( [TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing#264](https://github.com/TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing/issues/264) )

> [!WARNING]
> Please check that there no another web browser extension that has adblocking feature is enabled on your web browser. It can interfere AdGuard's filtering functionality.

> [!IMPORTANT]  
> Saving memory feature of your web browser or sleep feature of your operating system can interfere AdGuard's filtering functionality.
> In this case, please re-enable AdGuard's filtering by toggling the protection toggle.
> Following the instructions can be needed.

### The instructions
1. Force to update the filters by clicking the reload button in AdGuard Browser Extension/AdGuard mobile or update tray menu in your system tray. [^2]
2. Reload the YouTube webpage without cache by pressing <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>Shift</kbd> + <kbd>R</kbd> [^3].
3. Clear cache, cookies and site data of your web browser.
4. Re-open your web browser completely (checking your system monitor[^4] will be better).

[^1]: https://gitlab.com/adblockinc/ext/adblockplus/adblockplusui/-/issues/1576
[^2]: [AdGuard for Windows](https://adguard.com/kb/adguard-for-windows/overview/#tray-menu); [AdGuard for Mac](https://adguard.com/kb/adguard-for-mac/overview/#updates-check); 
[^3]: On macOS, <kbd>Command</kbd> + <kbd>Shift</kbd> + <kbd>R</kbd>
[^4]: Task Manager on Windows, System Monitor on GNOME Shell, KDE System Guard on KDE Plasma or Activity Monitor on macOS. 


Previous issue https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/issues/198973

comment.diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,84 @@
+name: Rotating Domains Checker
+
+on:
+  # Automatic run daily at 02:00 UTC
+  schedule:
+    - cron: '0 2 * * *'
+  # Manual run with mode selection
+  workflow_dispatch:
+    inputs:
+      mode:
+        description: 'Run mode'
+        required: false
+        default: 'prod_live'
+        type: choice
+        options:
+          - ''              # Use config.yml setting
+          - 'prod_live'     # Production mode with changes
+          - 'prod_dry'      # Production mode without changes (git operations simulation)
+          - 'test_live'     # Test directory with changes
+          - 'test_dry'      # Test directory without changes (git operations simulation)
+
+      config-path:
+        description: 'Path to configuration file'
+        required: false
+        default: './rdc_config.yml'
+        type: string
+
+      filters-path:
+        description: 'Path to filters directory'
+        required: false
+        default: './'
+        type: string
+
+jobs:
+  check-domains:
+    name: Rotating Domains Checker
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+
+    permissions:
+      contents: write          # Required for commits
+      pull-requests: write     # Required for PR creation
+      actions: read            # Required for reading workflow info
+
+    steps:
+      - name: 📥 Checkout repository
+        uses: actions/checkout@v4
+        with:
+          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
+          fetch-depth: 1
+
+      - name: 🔧 Setup Node.js
+        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
+        with:
+          node-version: '24'
+          cache: 'npm'
+
+      - name: 🚀 Run Rotating Domains Checker
+        run: |
+          # Configure git user for commits
+          git config --global user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
+          git config --global user.name "github-actions[bot]"
+
+          # Clone Rotating Domains Checker repository (public, no token needed)
+          git clone https://github.com/Alex-302/RotatingDomainsChecker.git /tmp/checker
+          cd /tmp/checker
+          npm install
+          npm run build
+
+          # Return to filters repository and run checker
+          cd $GITHUB_WORKSPACE
+          node /tmp/checker/dist/index.js

This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

issue.title: www.youtube.com

labels.name: T: Anti Adblock Script
labels.description: The site detects ad blocking

labels.name: NSFW
labels.description: Nudes and porn sites

labels.name: A: Problematic
labels.description: Issues, which cannot be resolved easily

issue.body:
# ATTENTION

> [!NOTE]  
> YouTube changes their detection scripts regularly. That means that sometime you can face the detection message.
> If all the following instructions do not work, please **email filters@adguard.com** or wait until any update is available. 

> [!CAUTION] 
> Please do not use another adblocker in the same time because using another adblocker can delay/lags the YouTube loading (e.g., AdBlock Plus and AdBlock)[^1] OR interfere AdGuard's filtering functionality.

> [!CAUTION] 
> Please do not use an userscript related to the bypassing YouTube detection message like [TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing](https://github.com/TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing).
> Recently, YouTube anti-adblock script added infinite dummy advertisements to try to neutralize the userscript. ( [TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing#264](https://github.com/TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing/issues/264) )

> [!WARNING]
> Please check that there no another web browser extension that has adblocking feature is enabled on your web browser. It can interfere AdGuard's filtering functionality.

> [!IMPORTANT]  
> Saving memory feature of your web browser or sleep feature of your operating system can interfere AdGuard's filtering functionality.
> In this case, please re-enable AdGuard's filtering by toggling the protection toggle.
> Following the instructions can be needed.

### The instructions
1. Force to update the filters by clicking the reload button in AdGuard Browser Extension/AdGuard mobile or update tray menu in your system tray. [^2]
2. Reload the YouTube webpage without cache by pressing <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>Shift</kbd> + <kbd>R</kbd> [^3].
3. Clear cache, cookies and site data of your web browser.
4. Re-open your web browser completely (checking your system monitor[^4] will be better).

[^1]: https://gitlab.com/adblockinc/ext/adblockplus/adblockplusui/-/issues/1576
[^2]: [AdGuard for Windows](https://adguard.com/kb/adguard-for-windows/overview/#tray-menu); [AdGuard for Mac](https://adguard.com/kb/adguard-for-mac/overview/#updates-check); 
[^3]: On macOS, <kbd>Command</kbd> + <kbd>Shift</kbd> + <kbd>R</kbd>
[^4]: Task Manager on Windows, System Monitor on GNOME Shell, KDE System Guard on KDE Plasma or Activity Monitor on macOS. 


Previous issue https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/issues/198973

### Prerequisites

- [x] I checked the documentation and found no answer;
- [x] I checked to make sure that this issue has not already been filed;
- [x] This is not an ad/bug report.

### Problem description

These rules appear multiple times[ in the list.](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/BaseFilter/sections/adservers.txt)

`185.246.188.124$network`
`185.246.188.124$network`
`||analyticsq.com^`

### Proposed solution

Merge or remove rules if possible.

### Additional information

_No response_

issue.title: photos.google.com
issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [http://photos.google.com](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=http%3A%2F%2Fphotos.google.com) ### Comment > Hello AdGuard Support Team, > > I would like to report an issue with video playback in Google Photos when using AdGuard for Mac. > > **Environment:** > > * AdGuard for Mac: **2.18.0.2089 (release)** > * macOS: **26.2 (25C56)** > * Browser: **Microsoft Edge Version 144.0.3719.115 (Official build) (arm64)** > (same behavior observed in other browsers) > * AdGuard Browser Extension: installed, but the issue persists even when the extension is disabled > > **Issue description:** > > * Videos on Google Photos (`https://photos.google.com`) fail to play. > * The video shows a loading spinner for a long time and then displays the message: > *“An error occurred. Please try again later.”* > * This happens **only when the AdGuard for Mac application is active**. > * As soon as I **pause AdGuard for Mac**, videos start playing immediately and reliably. > > **Key observations:** > > * Disabling the AdGuard browser extension has **no effect**. > * Adding exceptions: > > * via the regular whitelist, > * and via **Advanced Settings** (including broad domains such as `*.google.com`, `*.googleusercontent.com`, `*.gstatic.com`) > **does not resolve the issue**. > * Changing filtering modes (e.g. Automatic Proxy) **does not help**. > * **YouTube works without any issues** while AdGuard is active. > * The issue is reproducible across browsers and profiles. > > **Steps to reproduce:** > > 1. Enable AdGuard for Mac. > 2. Open Google Photos and attempt to play any video → playback fails. > 3. Pause AdGuard for Mac and reload the page → video plays successfully. > > If needed, I am happy to **provide debug logs or additional diagnostic information**. > Please let me know the preferred way to collect and submit logs for further analysis. > > Thank you for your time and support. > > ### Screenshots
